summary: Ceraxia CodeBrigade has a working greenfield pipeline with contracts, role modules, templates, verification, review, reports, memory, injectable model guidance, replayable model guidance for deterministic contract tests, coordinated file-set synthesis for source/test sets, mandatory module-by-module synthesis contracts, executable module synthesis, first-class test module contracts, stale-test rejection for source-only model output, task-derived behavior marker validation for generated tests, verification-driven module synthesis repair with post-repair rerun, model-guided bounded runtime repair, semantic exact-replace repair, AST-guided Python return-expression, constant, and small function-body repair, semantic/domain/artifact quality gates for generated source/tests, scenario-plan review for user workflows, model-consumed GreenfieldReviewer blockers, architecture-guidance evidence checks, and a definition-of-done evidence matrix that records proof or missing evidence for every declared completion item. Greenfield success now requires accepted model synthesis instead of quietly passing on templates when the model is unavailable, while allowing a rejected single-module JSON response to be reconciled only when coordinated file-set model output, semantic quality, verification, and review already prove that path. Ceraxia's outer review gate now treats a passed greenfield project package as the authoritative review source for project_creation runs, so legacy source-mutation gates no longer block accepted greenfield work, and advisory file-set synthesis timeouts no longer block a project whose module synthesis, verification, review, and definition of done all pass. GreenfieldArchitect now synchronizes README command documentation from the actual run_commands and verification_commands contract after template and feature selection, preventing templates or feature overrides from drifting away from the launch/test contract. The live model trial harness allocates collision-free trial directories and records synthesis, verification, review, and ledger evidence; live trials exposed stale-test, feature-classification, project-type routing, module JSON, wrapper-verification, final-rerun, repair-guidance-consumption, frontend feature-substitution, outer-review legacy-gate, advisory file-set, code-payload JSON transport, health-only API collapse, browser-game scenario fallback, and architecture evidence path-suffix failure modes that are now covered by contract validation or task-derived features. A dedicated live repair trial harness now forces verification failures and records whether GreenfieldRepairWorker used bounded line/exact/AST/module repair; no-replay local-model repair trials now accept guided_remove_undefined_name_line, guided_replace_return_expression, and guided_replace_python_constant repairs without falling back to full module synthesis. No-replay local-model CLI, FastAPI health, FastAPI inventory operations, FastAPI maintenance work orders, static todo, Vite todo dashboard, Vite kanban board, browser game, sales data pipeline, local-agent router, and Telegram bot trials reached accepted status with verification, review, and model ledger passing. The remaining risk is breadth and depth: curated templates/features still provide the reliable baseline source, and arbitrary large project code synthesis needs broader live model-backed real-world trials before this objective is complete.

requirements.remaining_risk: Schema is still permissive with additionalProperties=true for forward compatibility.

greenfield_architect.py delegates module_sequence, milestones, synthesis_policy, per-module code_synthesis_contract, anti_stub_policy, source_files, test_files, and model guidance into GreenfieldImplementationWorker

greenfield_implementation_worker.py execute_module_synthesis_contracts calls the GreenfieldImplementationWorker model path per module and writes source only after JSON, path, requirement, test, placeholder, and workspace validation

greenfield_project.py execute_greenfield_project_brief accepts an injected guidance provider and passes it through architect, file-set synthesis, module synthesis, verification repair, and review instead of forcing a global model singleton
greenfield_project.py model_synthesis_blockers prevents greenfield success when per-module model synthesis is unavailable, rejected, blocked, skipped, or applies no module implementations

greenfield_project.py reconcile_module_synthesis_with_file_set can accept a rejected per-module JSON response only when the same path was produced by applied coordinated file-set synthesis, semantic quality passed, verification passed, and GreenfieldReview passed

greenfield_architect.py routes data/csv/pipeline/analytics tasks to automation/data templates before generic API keywords, preventing CLI JSON output requests from being misclassified as FastAPI services

greenfield_templates.py declares tests as first-class module_contracts for baseline greenfield templates, so model synthesis must treat generated tests as owned implementation work instead of passive scaffold files

greenfield_verification_worker.py can call verification_repair module synthesis with failure context when bounded guided/template repair is not applicable

requirements.remaining_risk: Implementation now has executable validated LLM file-set synthesis, mandatory module synthesis, first-class test contracts, stale-test rejection, task behavior marker validation, verification-driven synthesis repair with post-repair rerun, semantic/domain/artifact quality gates, scenario scoring, full executor model-synthesis coverage, accepted no-replay live-model CLI/FastAPI/static-todo/Vite-todo/Vite-kanban/browser-game/FastAPI-inventory/FastAPI-maintenance/sales-data/local-agent trials, and API/data/frontend/local-agent plus long-form operations dashboard integration trials. Arbitrary large code synthesis still needs broader successful live model-backed real-world synthesis trials.

evidence: greenfield_dependency_worker.py reads stack dependency_plan, checks package manager availability with Python interpreter fallback for pip, records manifests with ecosystem/sha/status, blocks unsafe install paths, records lockfile snapshots, and reports blockers

requirements.remaining_risk: Install execution remains explicit/allowlisted by design; default behavior records manifests without installing.

evidence: greenfield_verification_worker.py runs verification, calls GreenfieldRepairWorker guidance, applies bounded guided/template/synthesis repairs, reruns, and records stop_condition_evidence
greenfield_verification_worker.py accepts GreenfieldRepairWorker exact replace operations only when the target path is inside the workspace and old_text has exactly one match
greenfield_verification_worker.py accepts GreenfieldRepairWorker replace_return_expression operations only for Python files when the target function and current return AST match exactly

greenfield_verification_worker.py accepts GreenfieldRepairWorker replace_python_constant operations only for Python files when exactly one top-level assignment exists and the current value AST matches exactly
greenfield_verification_worker.py accepts GreenfieldRepairWorker replace_function_body operations only when the target function exists once and the current body AST matches old_body exactly

greenfield_verification_worker.py includes bounded workspace_file_snapshots in GreenfieldRepairWorker prompts so the model can derive old_text, old_expression, old_literal, or old_body from current code
greenfield_repair_live_trial.py runs isolated live no-replay GreenfieldRepairWorker repair trials for name_error, exact_replace, return_expression, constant, and function_body scenarios
greenfield_verification_worker.py purges workspace __pycache__ files after source repair so immediate reruns cannot reuse stale bytecode after same-length edits

focused_self_test.py covers missing template file repair, model-guided NameError line repair, model-guided semantic exact replace repair, AST-guided return-expression repair, AST-guided constant repair, AST-guided function-body repair, ambiguous exact replace blocking, AST mismatch blocking, final post-repair rerun, and repeated failure stop condition
requirements.remaining_risk: Repair execution now consumes GreenfieldRepairWorker JSON guidance for narrow runtime NameError line repairs, bounded exact text replacement for assertion-style semantic failures, AST-guided Python return-expression changes, AST-guided Python constant changes, AST-guided small function-body changes, and module synthesis fallback. Live no-replay line-removal, return-expression, constant, and function-body-style assertion repair trials are accepted; broader confidence still needs more varied exact-text and full module-synthesis repair choices on larger projects.

focused_self_test.py covers placeholder blocking and semantic review status

focused_self_test.py test_greenfield_project_executor_uses_injected_model_for_full_synthesis_path proves a model-provided file-set and per-module implementation can pass executor verification, GreenfieldReview, reports, and ledger checks

Ceraxia review_gate.py greenfield_project_acceptance_from_worker uses project_creation execution intent, implemented execution_result, applied module synthesis, passed verification, passed GreenfieldReview, passed definition of done, and absence of execution blockers as the outer review source for greenfield projects

greenfield_live_trial.py accepted no-replay local-model CLI, FastAPI health, FastAPI inventory operations, static todo, Vite todo dashboard, Vite kanban board, browser game, sales data pipeline, local-agent router, and Telegram bot runs with GreenfieldArchitect, GreenfieldImplementationWorker file-set synthesis, GreenfieldImplementationWorker module synthesis, GreenfieldRepairWorker where needed, and GreenfieldReviewer recorded in the model guidance ledger

Ceraxia verification_report.py uses greenfield_project.verification as the authoritative wrapper-level verification evidence for project_creation runs, preventing stale generic commands from blocking a passed greenfield project
requirements.remaining_risk: LLM file-set and module code generation are executable through the full greenfield executor and validated with semantic/domain quality gates, and successful greenfield runs require accepted model synthesis or narrowly reconciled file-set coverage with passed verification/review. No-replay live model CLI, FastAPI health, FastAPI inventory operations, FastAPI maintenance work orders, static todo, Vite todo dashboard, Vite kanban board, browser game, sales data pipeline, local-agent router, Telegram bot, and multiple guided repair trials are accepted; the system still needs broader live real-world scenario coverage before it can rely on arbitrary synthesis as the primary implementation path.

evidence: greenfield_memory_worker.py records stack, dependencies, verification, stop evidence, repaired files, review status, acceptance features, scenario status, and reusable learnings
greenfield_memory_worker.py records definition_of_done evidence rows from GreenfieldReview so future runs can learn which completion proof was missing
greenfield_project.py writes greenfield_memory_record.json and greenfield_run_report.json after each run
focused_self_test.py test_greenfield_memory_worker_records_definition_of_done_evidence_matrix verifies DoD evidence/missing_evidence is preserved in memory
focused_self_test.py and Ceraxia self tests verify report artifacts
requirements.remaining_risk: Memory is file-based per run; no long-term retrieval/ranking layer is implemented yet.

next_recommended_work: Run broader live no-replay greenfield trials for larger cross-stack projects and require source, tests, verification, review, and model ledger to pass together.

Run additional live no-replay repair trials on larger projects that force GreenfieldRepairWorker to choose exact-text, multi-file function-body, and module-synthesis repairs from real verification failures.
Decide whether Android/game templates belong in this goal now or should remain explicitly deferred.
